findings.title: Offer creation stores the real order under a hidden ID while every public interface returns and emits `0`

best_agent_match.title: Token accounting assumes exact ERC20 transfers and can leave ETH trades underpaid or HEX offers undercollateralized

findings.title: Using Solidity `transfer` for ETH payouts lets contract wallets permanently lock or DOS ETH-backed trades

best_agent_match.title: Directly transferred HEX or forcibly sent ETH can become permanently stranded in the contract

findings.title: The OTC blindly binds to a hardcoded HEX address, so a wrong-chain deployment can settle against attacker-controlled token code

best_agent_match.title: Directly transferred HEX or forcibly sent ETH can become permanently stranded in the contract

rejected_candidates.title: Token accounting assumes exact ERC20 transfers and can leave ETH trades underpaid or HEX offers undercollateralized
rejected_candidates.reason: Speculative for the intended hardcoded HEX token, which is not shown here to be fee-on-transfer or rebasing; if the bound token is malicious or nonstandard on another chain, that risk is already covered by F-003.

rejected_candidates.title: Orders are globally fillable by any observer, so negotiated OTC trades can be sniped before the intended counterparty
rejected_candidates.reason: This contract implements a public on-chain order book with no taker binding or signature scheme; first-come-first-served fills are the explicit design, not a code-level vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Makers can self-fill their own orders and emit successful trade events without performing a real swap
rejected_candidates.reason: The behavior can spoof logs, but it does not by itself create realistic protocol-level fund loss, insolvency, lockup, or permissionless DoS in this codebase.

rejected_candidates.title: Directly transferred HEX or forcibly sent ETH can become permanently stranded in the contract
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic accidental-transfer limitation requiring user error or forced ETH; it is not an exploitable protocol bug and does not create meaningful incremental risk beyond standard unsolicited-balance behavior.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked `last_offer_id++` can eventually wrap and reuse order IDs
rejected_candidates.reason: Technically true in Solidity 0.4.x, but practically unreachable and therefore not reportable.
